name: numpy
use: n-dimensional arrays, linear algebra, vectorized math
notes: foundation for most scientific Python; prefer vectorized ops over Python loops

name: scipy
use: optimization, integration, signal/stats, sparse linear algebra
example: from scipy import optimize; optimize.minimize(f, x0)
notes: scipy.stats for hypothesis tests; scipy.sparse for large systems

name: pandas
notes: use categorical dtypes + vectorized ops for large frames

name: scikit-learn
use: classical ML: regression, classification, clustering, model selection
notes: always report cross-validated metrics; fix random_state for reproducibility

name: statsmodels
example: import statsmodels.api as sm; sm.OLS(y, X).fit().summary()
notes: use when you need inferential statistics, not just prediction

name: networkx
notes: for very large graphs consider igraph/graph-tool

name: sympy
use: symbolic math: algebra, calculus, equation solving
example: import sympy as sp; sp.integrate(sp.sin(x), x)
notes: use for exact/closed-form derivations and codegen

name: matplotlib
use: plotting/figures (also see the paper-plot / nature-figure packs)
example: import matplotlib.pyplot as plt; plt.plot(x, y)
notes: for submission figures prefer the nature-figure adapter conventions
